magicitems.destroyFlavorText: reaches 0 charges: it crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

description.value:
Your devotion and enlightenment allow you to channel your power through the divine power to whom you addresses your prayers. Priest’s spell list includes Cleric and Ranger spell lists. The spell list may be modified with GMs and players in agreement.

CANTRIPS
At 1st level, you know two cantrips of your choice. You learn another cantrip at 6th, 14th and 18th levels.

SPELL SLOT
You have a single 1st level spell slot. At 2nd level you acquire a second slot, at 11th level you acquire a third slot and at 17th level you acquire a fourth slot. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short rest. All of your spell slots are always of the same level, which varies according to the chosen Divine Way.

KNOWN SPELLS
At 1st level, you know two 1st-level spells. Each time you acquire a level as a priest, you can learn one more spell, up to a maximum of 12 spells when you reach the 11th level. You can replace a spell you know when you gain a new priest level and at the end of a long rest. You can only acquire spells for which you have an appropriate level spell slot.

SPELLCASTING ABILITY
Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your priest spells. The power of your spells comes from your devotion to your deity. You use your Wisdom whenever a priest spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Wisdom modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a Cleric spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

Spell Saving Throw DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus
+ your Wisdom modifier

Spell Attack Modifier = your proficiency bonus
+ your Wisdom modifier

SPELLCASTING FOCUS
You can use a sacred symbol as a spellcasting focus for your priest spells.

LevelKnown SpellsCantirp
